title: Take My Life And Let It Be

%%combinevoices 1

% 18
W: 6.Take my love, my Lord, I pour at Thy feet its treasure store.
W: Take myself, and I will be ever, only, all for Thee.

metadata.composer: 'Mozart' Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart (1756-1791) composite found in "Hymnal and Liturgies of the Moravian Church", 1920
metadata.copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2008 Revision.
c_lines: Words: Frances R. Havergal, 1874.  Music: 'Mozart' Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart (1756-1791).
Setting: composite found in "Hymnal and Liturgies of the Moravian Church", 1920.
copyright: public domain.  This score is a part of the Open Hymnal Project, 2008 Revision.

1.raw_text: Take my life, and let it be consecrated, Lord, to Thee. Take my moments and my days; let them flow in ceaseless praise.

2.raw_text: Take my hands, and let them move at the impulse of Thy love. Take my feet, and let them be swift and beautiful for Thee.

3.raw_text: Take my voice, and let me sing always, only, for my King. Take my lips, and let them be filled with messa- ges from Thee.

4.raw_text: Take my silver and my gold; not a mite would I withhold. Take my intellect, and use every pow'r as Thou shalt choose.

5.raw_text: Take my will, and make it Thine; it shall be no longer mine. Take my heart, it is Thine own; it shall be Thy royal throne.
